title: anthriscus vulgaris subsp. hispanica
intro: Anthriscus vulgaris subsp. hispanica, also known as Spanish parsley or cumin, is a biennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant has been used for centuries due to its medicinal properties, particularly in aiding digestion and reducing inflammation. The health benefits of Anthriscus vulgaris subsp. hispanica include its ability to alleviate symptoms of indigestion, diarrhea, and irritable bowel syndrome, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of this plant often come in the form of teas, tinctures, or infused oils, which can be used to treat various health issues.
